steps.title: Subtract to find the sum of the other three resistors
steps.content:
The 5 resistors have a combined resistance of 120 ohms. Two of these resistors sum to 75 ohms. The remaining three resistors must sum to:
$$120 - 75 = 45 \text{ ohms}$$

steps.title: Apply positivity constraints to determine the range for x
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the resistance of one of the other 3 resistors, and let the other two have resistances $r$ and $s$. Then:
$$x + r + s = 45$$
Since each resistor must have positive resistance:
  • $x \gt 0$ (the resistor itself must be positive)
  • $r \gt 0$ and $s \gt 0$ (the other two must be positive)
Since $r + s = 45 - x$ and we need $r + s \gt 0$, we must have $45 - x \gt 0$, which means $x \lt 45$.
Therefore: $0 \lt x \lt 45$. B is correct.
